### Hello there 👋🏼

I'm **Caelan Miller**, a current Computational & Systems Biology PhD Candidate [@WUSTL](https://dbbs.wustl.edu/programs/computational-system-biology/) in the [Dantas Lab](https://dantaslab.wustl.edu/) 🦠💊

I develop reproducible computational pipelines, manage lab HPC infrastructure, and build Python tools for microbial genomic analyses. I'm also pursuing **AAS in Network Security Engineering & Cybersecurity** to complement my computational work.


## 🔬 Research Interests

- Microbial Genomics  
- Antimicrobial Resistance  
- Bioinformatics Workflow Automation  
- Scientific Software Development  


## 🎓 Education & Training

- PhD Candidate, Computational & Systems Biology, [Washington University in St. Louis](https://dbbs.wustl.edu) 
- AAS in [Network Security Engineering](https://stlcc.edu/pathways/stem/network-security-engineering) (in progress)
- AAS in [Cybersecurity](https://stlcc.edu/pathways/stem/cybersecurity/) (in progress)

## 🧬 Bioinformatics & HPC Work

I develop and maintain **software infrastructure for HPC environments**, focusing on **reproducible, containerized workflows and environment management**.  

Key activities include:

- Managing and automating lab-scale HPC workflows  
- Designing Python tools and custom data structures for bacterial isolate datasets  
- Creating reproducible computational pipelines  
- Handling Linux-based environments, Docker containers, Conda environments, and [Spack](https://github.com/spack/spack)-managed software  
- Network security & cybersecurity fundamentals, emphasizing secure infrastructure and environment management (AAS in progress)
